top.value: There is a budget for evaluation within each of the six operational programmes in order to tailor the evaluations to the specific contexts and focus issues of the programmes. Evaluations on an overall level will be conducted within the methods programme. The lessons drawn from evaluations will inform decisions and planning regarding ongoing interventions. The costs for evaluations are divided equally between the years but will most likely be conducted later in the programme period.Project OrganisationsThe project is implemented by the Palme Center.Project ObjectiveTo evaluate outcomes/behavioural changes in relation to programme objectives and to draw lessons learned which will inform decisions and planning regarding ongoing interventions.

The purpose of the Palme Centers monitoring is to ensure that the programme teams have as accurate information as possible about outcomes, to enable well-founded analysis and learnings. The analysis and learnings shall facilitate the ongoing adaptation and improvement of the programmes and continuously test the relevance of the programmes idea on how change happen. Contextually relevant and cost-effective programmes are essential for being accountable to the rights holders. Furthermore, the analysis and learning are important to be able to create qualitative financial and narrative reports, which help us to be accountable towards the board and the donors. Finally, gathering information about the outcomes of the programmes is very helpful when communicating what the Palme Center does and contributes to. The project includes monitoring travels and related costs.Project OrganisationsThe project is implemented by the Palme Center.Project ObjectiveThe aim of the project is to follow-up and assure the quality of the projects and programmes, track outcomes and changes in relation to the programme objective, support and strengthen the capacity of the partner organisations.
The main outcome areas it contributes to are democracy and Human Rights, Gender Equality, Just Transition, Peace and Dialogue

Projektledningen relaterar till den tid som går åt till att kvalitetssäkra projekten. Detta sker utöver monitoring/uppföljningsresor även i form av strategidiskussioner, partnerskapsfrämjande insatser, rådgivning och bedömning under projektcykelns gång, kapacitetsutveckling, dialog kring de regelverk som styr anslaget och uppdateringar av förändringar i kontexten.

The local offices of the Palme Center is a vital part of our work and operations and is established in a number of countries based on a strategic decision to increase presence to meet our operational needs.

The project is implemented in South Africa. The main outcome areas it contributes to are gender equality and democracy and human rights. The main rights holders are women, youth and elderly people. Through counselling, workshops and campaigns the project aims to achieve progressive development within the outcome areas.

Pre-studies aim to carry out feasibility assessments at an early stage of possible projects, operations and/or partners to support.Project OrganisationsThe project is an internal project implemented by the Olof Palme International Center, and often in cooperation with a Member organisation.Project ObjectiveThe objective is that the Palme Center has sufficient knowledge to decide whether to support a project, new operations and new a partnership.

OPC applies a zero-tolerance policy on corruption. It is in this premise that capacity building is the central point of its efforts and initiatives with focus on strengthening accountability, participation and transparency among its partner-organisations and member-organisations. The project focuses on the conduct of regular and up-to-date anti-corruption seminars such as fraud awareness/detection and strengthening existing partner-organisations finance and administrative system. Part of OPC's anti-corruption policy is the inclusion of procurement and code of conduct steering documents that are regularly revisited and form part of all cooperation agreements. In addition to developing system and procedures to counter corruption, there is a specific focus on awareness raising with regards to nepotism, cronyism and power relations. OPC's work in this regard is centered around values and expected behaviour rather than a list of consequences related to specific transgressions.Project OrganisationsThis is a Palme Center project.Other Donors to the OrganisationNo other donors involved in this project.Project ObjectiveThe main objective of the project is to contribution in the fight against corruption by establishing, supporting and contributing to changes, more transparent and democratic organisational structures and behavioural patterns. OPC believes that the labour movement and civil society have a central role to play as role models, democractic schools and agents of change in the work against corruption.

Funds for non-planned projects that enable activities to be developed during the programme period in order to contribute to the programme objectives. In a changing and complex environment, there is a need for flexibility and available funds to expand the operations with new/innovative initiatives with relevant partners in the programmes. Existing partner organisations may also have well-motivated reasons for increasing their budgets.Project OrganisationsThe project is implemented by the Palme Center.Other Donors to the OrganisationThe aim of the project is to enable new and innovative initiatives with relevant partners during the programme period to meet a complex and changing context.

Human Rights Association in Istanbul (HRA from now on) continues to monitor human rights violations. They followed up 491 violations, had 20 internal trainings, 12 public panels and 209 press conferences.Despite threats and attacks, (HRA from now on) continued their activities. During 2023, trainings, documentation, reporting, information, and awareness-raising activities were carried out. During special days, as Pride Week, 8 March Womens Day, Workers Day and so on, HRA reached over 1 million people through social media and direct contact with about 15000 people.The earthquake had of course effect on HRA as well as the threats that has continued and has been a stress situation for the staff and activists. HRA continues to request meetings with governorship and different ministries and there has been some result regarding some decisions that were partially lifted.The solidarity between institutions have increased, but this is not something that goes out publicly due to prevent negative reactions. The political and economic atmosphere in Trkiye is still difficult and HRA feels the anxiety among the rights holders, they have tried to reduce this anxiety by continuing the activities.
